description.content:
Instances are the virtual machines at the core of the Exoscale
                   Compute service.

item.name: Create a Snapshot of a Compute instance

request.name: Create a Snapshot of a Compute instance

item.name: Reveal the password used during instance creation or the latest password reset.

request.name: Reveal the password used during instance creation or the latest password reset.

description.content:
Reveal the password used during instance creation or the latest password reset.
            This is only available for VMs created against templates having the `password-enabled`
            property set to `true`.

            Passwords are transiently stored for at most 24 hours and intended to be retrieved shortly after
            creation or resets.
This operation resizes a Compute instance's disk volume. Note: the disk can only grow, cannot be shrunk.
This operation starts a virtual machine, potentially using a rescue profile if specified

item.name: Scale a Compute instance to a new Instance Type

request.name: Scale a Compute instance to a new Instance Type

description.content: This operation changes the Compute instance's type. Note: the new Instance Type must be within the same family (e.g. a standard instance cannot be scaled to gpu2 or storage).

item.name: Reset a Compute instance to a base/target template

request.name: Reset a Compute instance to a base/target template

description.content: This operation re-installs a Compute instance to a base template. If target template is provided it will be used to recreated instance from. Warning: the operation wipes all data stored on the disk.

item.name: Retrieve signed url valid for 60 seconds to connect via console-proxy websocket to VM VNC console.

request.name: Retrieve signed url valid for 60 seconds to connect via console-proxy websocket to VM VNC console.

item.name: Revert a snapshot for an instance

request.name: Revert a snapshot for an instance

description.content:
This operation reverts the snapshot to the Compute instance volume, restoring stored data as it was at the time of the snapshot.
The Compute instance must be previously stopped.
Kafka is a distributed, open-source data platform for optimized real-time processing of streaming data.

A Zone represents an independent datacenter in which Exoscale
                   infrastructure is deployed into.
Databases as a Service (DBaaS) provides scalable and fully-managed database solutions with high availability and performance backed by Exoscale's Compute and Storage services.

description.content:
Security Groups are groups of firewall rules that regulate
                   network traffic to and from your Compute instances.

Lists security groups. When visibility is set to public, lists public security groups.
Public security groups are objects maintained by Exoscale which contain source addresses for
relevant services hosted by Exoscale. They can be used a source in ingress rules and as a destination
in egress rules.

description.content:
Events form the basis of the Exoscale audit-trail, a mechanism
                   to query past events performing mutations on resources
                   which happened on an organization.

Retrieve Mutation Events for a given date range. Defaults to retrieving Events for the past 24 hours.
         Both a `from` and `to` arguments can be specified to filter Events over a specific period.
         Events will be the the most descriptive possible but not all fields are mandatory

Lifecycle of AI Models.

Model files will be downloaded from Huggingface.

Name must be the exact name of the model on huggingface (ex: openai/gpt-oss-120b or ggml-org/gpt-oss-120b-GGUF).

If the model is under a license then you must provide a Huggingface access token for an account that signed the license agreement
